navigation_path.description: First text field in the sign-in form, located on the left of the first input row.
Second text field in the sign-in form, located to the right of the email/username field.
Primary action button on the right side below the password field.
Secondary action button to the left of the Sign In button on the same row.
Checkbox below the input fields on the left side of the form.
Link below the divider and under the 'Don't have a Disney account?' text.
Logo link centered above the sign-in panel near the top of the page.
Link aligned to the right of the password label, above the password field.

navigation_path.element: Information icon/help control for Email Address or Username
navigation_path.description: Small icon adjacent to the email/username label, visually associated with that field.
First footer link at the bottom left area of the page.
Second footer link to the right of Help & Guest Services.
Third footer link in the bottom row.
Fourth footer link in the bottom row.
Fifth footer link in the bottom row.

navigation_path.element: Do Not Sell My Info link
navigation_path.description: Sixth footer link in the bottom row.
Seventh footer link in the bottom row.
Eighth footer link at the far right of the footer.

lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Starting focus in the first form field is acceptable because it places the keyboard user at the primary interactive content of the page.
Moving from the left email/username field to the right password field follows the expected left-to-right order within the same row.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the password field to the Sign In button before other controls that are earlier in visual reading order, including the Remember Me checkbox on the left and the Cancel button to the left of Sign In.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: Under a top-to-bottom, left-to-right reading model, after the password field the next relevant controls on the next row would be Remember Me on the left and then Cancel before Sign In. Jumping directly to the rightmost primary button disrupts the linear sequence and does not preserve the visual/meaningful order of the form controls.
lnf_analysis.impact: Keyboard users encounter actions before supporting options and before the left-side checkbox, making the form feel disjointed and increasing the chance they miss available choices.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: This jump skips earlier-positioned controls and breaks the expected left-to-right, top-to-bottom reading order.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus reverses direction within the same row by moving from the rightmost Sign In button back to the left-side Cancel button.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: Within a single horizontal row, logical reading order is left to right; Cancel appears before Sign In visually, so encountering Sign In first and then moving backward to Cancel is not intuitive.
lnf_analysis.impact: Keyboard users may misinterpret the structure of the action row and can activate the primary action before discovering the secondary action.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The focus order moves right-to-left across the action buttons, which is opposite the expected reading sequence.

lnf_analysis.issue: After reaching the Cancel button on the right, focus jumps leftward and slightly upward to the Remember Me checkbox.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: Remember Me is visually earlier in the row and should have been reached before the action buttons; placing it after both buttons creates a non-linear sequence that does not match how a sighted user would consume the form.
lnf_analysis.impact: Users may overlook the checkbox until after passing the submit/cancel actions, reducing form comprehension and efficiency.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The checkbox is visually positioned before the buttons but is reached only after them, creating an illogical backward jump.
Moving from the Remember Me checkbox down to the Create Account link follows the general top-to-bottom flow of the page.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus jumps from the lower Create Account link back up to the top-page Walt Disney World logo link.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: The logo is visually above the form and should be encountered before the form controls if it is in the tab order; reaching it only after a lower form section breaks spatial and meaningful order.
lnf_analysis.impact: Keyboard users are forced to revisit upper-page content after progressing downward, which is disorienting and interrupts task flow.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: This is a major upward jump to content that is visually much earlier on the page.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the top logo down into the Forgot password? link, but bypasses the main form sequence and lands on a label-adjacent utility link late in the order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: Forgot password? is visually associated with the password label area near the top of the form and should be encountered around the password field, not after lower-page content and the top logo.
lnf_analysis.impact: This makes the page feel unpredictably ordered and may cause users to miss contextual assistance when it would be most useful.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The link is contextually tied to the password area but is reached far later than its visual position suggests.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the Forgot password? link on the right to the information icon near the email label on the left, effectively jumping upward and leftward within an earlier part of the form.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: The info icon is visually located near the first field label and should appear before or around the email field in a logical reading sequence, not after lower controls, the logo, and the forgot-password link.
lnf_analysis.impact: Users encounter supplementary help for the first field only after most of the page has already been traversed, which undermines comprehension and predictability.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: This transition returns to an earlier, upper-left contextual control that should have appeared near the start of the form.
After the final upper-page control, moving down to the first footer link is a reasonable continuation toward the bottom of the page.
The footer links proceed left to right in a standard horizontal sequence.
This follows the visible left-to-right order of adjacent footer links.
The focus order remains consistent with the displayed footer link sequence.
This is a normal left-to-right progression through the footer navigation.
The transition preserves the visible order of footer items.
Focus continues logically across the footer links from left to right.
This is the expected final left-to-right step within the footer row.
Exiting to browser chrome after the last page focusable element is expected and does not indicate a page-level focus-order problem.
